title: Tandem RNA and Protein Extraction from Limited Ex Vivo Tissue Samples
author: Ciarán Kennedy
license_note: PMC full-text XML — Bio-protocol — CC-BY confirmed in article XML

Homogenize the tissue in a QIAGEN TissueLyser II for 10 min at 25 oscillations per second. Note: Ensure the tissue is fully homogenized before continuing to step B

Proceed with protein quantification or store for downstream applications (-80 °C). Note: Suitable assays for protein quantification will depend on the composition of the final buffer in which the sample is dissolved. For example, the standard Bradford assay is incompatible with detergents (e.g., 1% SDS or Laemmli buffer), while the BCA assay is incompatible with reducing agents (e.g., DTT or 2-mercaptoethanol). The Bio-Rad’s detergent-compatible Bradford assay is compatible with moderate concentrations of detergent and reducing agent.

steps.title: Human tissue sample, e
steps.instruction: Human tissue sample, e.g., carotid endarterectomy (approximately 3 × 3 × 3 mm in size or 15–30 mg in weight)

steps.title: Animal tissue sample, e
steps.instruction: Animal tissue sample, e.g., snap-frozen mouse aorta (tissue samples of 15–30 mg, including small organs, can be processed whole if the tissue thickness does not exceed 5 mm)

steps.title: 70% ethanol solution Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: 70% ethanol solution Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume Ethanol 70% 70 mL Ultrapure water - 30 mL Store at 4 °C.

steps.title: TRK lysis buffer with 2% β-mercaptoethanol...
steps.instruction: TRK lysis buffer with 2% β-mercaptoethanol Reagent Final Concentration Quantity or volume TRK lysis buffer (or equivalent) - 9.8 mL β-mercaptoethanol 2% 200 µL Store at 4 °C. Stable for one month. Laboratory supplies

steps.title: Pipette filter tips (P2–P
steps.instruction: Pipette filter tips (P2–P

steps.title: Place tissue sections into a labeled 1
steps.instruction: Place tissue sections into a labeled 1.5 mL centrifuge tube containing 300 μL of pre-chilled RNA later and store at 4 °C at least overnight

steps.title: The following day, aspirate off the RNA later
steps.instruction: The following day, aspirate off the RNA later .

steps.title: Proceed immediately to homogenization of tissue...
steps.instruction: Proceed immediately to homogenization of tissue or store tissue for later use at -20 °C or below. A

steps.title: Place frozen tissue sections into a labeled 1
steps.instruction: Place frozen tissue sections into a labeled 1.5 mL centrifuge tube containing 300 μL of pre-chilled RNA later -ICE solution and incubate at -20 °C for at least 16 h.

steps.title: For storage, keep the samples in...
steps.instruction: For storage, keep the samples in RNA later -ICE and store at -20 °C or below. When ready to process, aspirate off the RNA later -ICE and proceed immediately to homogenization. Samples are stable in RNA later -ICE for up to 30 min at room temperature. Notes:

steps.title: Duplicate/triplicate tissues can be grouped together...
steps.instruction: Duplicate/triplicate tissues can be grouped together and placed in the same solution of RNA later without changing the volume of RNA later in the tube, as the existing volume is sufficient to cover the tissue.

steps.title: RNA in RNA later -treated samples...
steps.instruction: RNA in RNA later -treated samples is stable at 4 °C for one month and at -20 °C (or lower) indefinitely.

steps.title: RNA in samples treated with RNA...
steps.instruction: RNA in samples treated with RNA later -ICE is stable for at least 16 h at 4 °C and at -20 °C (or lower) indefinitely (as long as the tissue remains submerged in RNA later -ICE).

steps.title: The use of RNA later -ICE...
steps.instruction: The use of RNA later -ICE can dry out the snap-frozen tissue, making accurate determination of tissue weight difficult. A

steps.title: Collect up to 5 × 10...
steps.instruction: Collect up to 5 × 10 6 cells by centrifugation and remove residual medium or PBS by aspiration.

steps.title: Add 350 μL of TRK lysis...
steps.instruction: Add 350 μL of TRK lysis buffer, supplemented with 2% β-mercaptoethanol, mix thoroughly, and incubate at room temperature for 5 min.

steps.title: Add an equal volume of 70%...
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume of 70% ethanol and proceed to section C. B. Tissue homogenization

steps.title: Weigh out the tissue in a...
steps.instruction: Weigh out the tissue in a homogenizer tube (optional) before adding a stainless-steel homogenizer bead and 350 μL of TRK lysis buffer with 2% β-mercaptoethanol. Notes:

steps.title: Pre-chill TRK lysis buffer with 2%...
steps.instruction: Pre-chill TRK lysis buffer with 2% β-mercaptoethanol at 4 °C for at least 30 min before use.

steps.title: Add lysis buffer with β-mercaptoethanol under the chemical hood
steps.instruction: Add lysis buffer with β-mercaptoethanol under the chemical hood.

steps.title: The blocks from the homogenizer can...
steps.instruction: The blocks from the homogenizer can be chilled at -80 °C before use if desired (optional).

steps.title: If necessary, return to the homogenizer and repeat step B
steps.instruction: If necessary, return to the homogenizer and repeat step B

steps.title: Transfer the homogenized tissue in the...
steps.instruction: Transfer the homogenized tissue in the lysis buffer into a 1.5 mL centrifuge tube and centrifuge at 12,000 g for 5 min at 4 °C to collect cell debris.

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant into a new, pre-labeled centrifuge tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant into a new, pre-labeled centrifuge tube. Note: Some samples may contain more fat and debris than others. A second centrifugation is recommended if you are unable to aspirate off the supernatant without disturbing the pellet, as the debris can clog the RNA extraction column membrane.

steps.title: Add an equal volume of chilled...
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume of chilled 70% ethanol to the transferred supernatant and mix thoroughly (final volume of approximately 700 μL). C. RNA extraction Note: If using a different column-based RNA extraction kit, follow the manufacturer's instructions for RNA isolation and retain the initial column flowthrough for protein extraction. Some optimization may be required to collect additional wash flowthroughs or adjust the volume of ethanol used for protein precipitation.

steps.title: Transfer the entire homogeneous solution (approximately...
steps.instruction: Transfer the entire homogeneous solution (approximately 700 μL), including any precipitate that may have formed, into an RNA extraction column placed inside a collection tube and centrifuge at 10,000× g for 1 min at 4 °C. Note: Retain the initial flowthrough from step C1, as it will be used in section D.

steps.title: Move the column to a new...
steps.instruction: Move the column to a new 2 mL collection tube, add 500 μL of wash buffer I from the EZNA extraction kit, and centrifuge at 10,000× g for 30 s at 4 °C. Note: Pre-chill the RNA extraction reagents (wash buffer I and II, 70% ethanol, and RNase-free water) at least 30 min before use.

steps.title: Discard the flowthrough and add 500...
steps.instruction: Discard the flowthrough and add 500 μL of wash buffer II before centrifuging at 10,000× g for 1 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Centrifuge the spin column at maximum...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the spin column at maximum speed (≥14,000× g ) for 2 min at 4 °C in a clean 2 mL collection tube to dry the spin column membrane.

steps.title: Transfer the spin column to a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the spin column to a 1.5 mL centrifuge tube and then add 20–40 μL of RNase-free water to the membrane.

steps.title: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 2 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 2 min at 4 °C. 8. (Optional) Collect the eluted RNA and add it back to the membrane again before centrifuging at maximum speed for 2 min at 4 °C again. Note: This step can help increase the RNA concentration in the eluted sample and ensure that all RNA is eluted from the column.

steps.title: Place the centrifuge tube containing the...
steps.instruction: Place the centrifuge tube containing the eluted RNA on ice and quantify the RNA concentration using a nanodrop spectrophotometer or store at -80 °C. D. Protein extraction

steps.title: Transfer the flowthrough obtained in step...
steps.instruction: Transfer the flowthrough obtained in step C1 into a pre-labeled 1.5 mL centrifuge tube and add an equal volume of ice-cold 100% ethanol.

steps.title: Invert or vortex the tube until the solution is homogeneous
steps.instruction: Invert or vortex the tube until the solution is homogeneous.

steps.title: Store the labeled tube upright at...
steps.instruction: Store the labeled tube upright at -20 °C for at least 1 h (or preferably overnight) to precipitate the protein.

steps.title: Briefly vortex the sample (2–3 s)...
steps.instruction: Briefly vortex the sample (2–3 s) to break up any large chunks of precipitate, which may prevent future resuspension.

steps.title: Centrifuge precipitated protein at 2,000× g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge precipitated protein at 2,000× g for 3 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Carefully decant or pipette off the...
steps.instruction: Carefully decant or pipette off the supernatant without disturbing the pellet (a small volume of supernatant can be left behind to avoid pellet disruption).

steps.title: Fully resuspend the pellet in 500...
steps.instruction: Fully resuspend the pellet in 500 μL of ice-cold 70% ethanol, being careful to include any precipitate that may have settled on the wall of the tube. Note: During this step, the pellet must be fully resuspended to ensure that the samples will dissolve later.

steps.title: Centrifuge resuspended protein at 2,000× g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge resuspended protein at 2,000× g for 3 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Fully resuspend the pellet in 500...
steps.instruction: Fully resuspend the pellet in 500 μL of ice-cold 70% ethanol again.

steps.title: Centrifuge resuspended protein at 5,000× g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge resuspended protein at 5,000× g for 5 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Remove as much supernatant as possible...
steps.instruction: Remove as much supernatant as possible and allow residual 70% ethanol to evaporate. Note: Air dry or use a vacuum aspirator inside the lid of the tube, not touching the sample, to evaporate the ethanol. Take care not to over-dry, as this will prevent proper dissolution of the protein pellet. Do not use a Speed-vac or other vacuum concentrator.

steps.title: Dissolve the pellet in an appropriate...
steps.instruction: Dissolve the pellet in an appropriate buffer for downstream applications, e.g., for a western blot, 1× Laemmli buffer with DTT. Samples may require heating and/or vortexing to be fully dissolved.

description: Validation of protocol To validate the robustness of this protocol, we performed tandem RNA and protein extraction from human CEA tissue (n ≥ 12) ( Figures 1 – 2 ), ApoE-/- mouse aorta tissue (n ≥ 12) ( Figure 3A ), and THP-1 monocytes and macrophages (n ≥ 12) ( Figure 3B , C). Each replicate of CEA tissue, mouse aorta, and cell lysates was analyzed using an RNA bioanalyser to assess RNA quality. The precipitated proteins were subsequently analyzed by western blotting; total protein staining was performed to evaluate sample quality and usability. Representative examples are displayed here. Figure 1. Assessment of protein integrity and responsiveness of ex vivo–treated human carotid plaque tissue. Plaques were stimulated with 5 ng/mL TNF for 24 h, after which both protein and RNA were extracted and analyzed. (A) Fluorescent total protein staining, showing suitable protein distribution and integrity. Proteins were resolved on a 4%–12% SDS-PAGE gel and transferred onto a PVDF membrane using an Invitrogen PowerBlotter system. Following transfer, total protein was stained using the reversible total protein stain Revert 700. Representative imaging was acquired using the LICOR Odyssey CLX imaging system. (B) Membranes were incubated with a primary antibody against phosphorylated p44/p42 MAPK (Erk1/2) (Cell Signaling Technology, #9101) and developed using the LICOR Odyssey CLX imaging system following incubation with an appropriate fluorescently tagged secondary antibody (DyLight 680 conjugated anti-rabbit, Invitrogen, #35569). TNF stimulation increased phosphorylation of ERK1/2 by western blot in plaque tissue after 24 h, indicating tissue viability, responsiveness to external stimuli, and suitability for downstream molecular analyses. Each replicate represents tissue from an individual patient's coronary endarterectomy. (C) Quantification of (B), performed using LICOR Image Studio 6 and normalizing the signal intensity of the protein of interest to the total protein signa
